patches.note: Panama City: 'Ramal línea 2 : Corredor Sur → Aeropuerto' (OSM relation 15624911) is the 3-station airport branch of Line 2 (shares Corredor Sur with the main L2 route; ITSE/Aeropuerto are its own stations). Upstream OSM tags have no colour and no ref, so it grouped as its own untagged line and fell back to the default red — visually indistinguishable from Line 1. Wikipedia/mapa-metro.com confirm the airport branch is operationally part of Line 2 and shares its green livery. Setting ref=L2 lets it merge into Line 2's group via the normal same-network+ref grouping (colour then inherited from the coloured variant); colour is set directly too as belt-and-braces. Remove once upstream OSM adds these tags.

Bursaray B2 route_master (r7869622, ref=B2 BursaRay 2): OSM tags colour=black, a placeholder (no line is black). The master colour overrides the variant colours, so the patch must target the master, not the route relations 7869620/7869621. Bursaray is one branching system sharing an eastern trunk; per user, both branches use B1's red. Setting colour=red (→ #e6194b). Remove if upstream OSM sets a real colour.
